item.title: All NASA News - NASA
item.description: Stay up-to-date with the latest content from NASA as we explore the universe and discover more about our home planet.

item.title: NASA Media Contacts - NASA
item.description: Media Contacts Gerelle DodsonNASA Workforce, STEM Engagement202-358-1600Jennifer DoorenDeputy News Chief, Cybersecurity202-358-1600Alise

item.title: NASA News
item.description: Resources for news media, including NASA news contacts and archived news material

item.title: NASA Says Farewell to MAVEN Mars Mission, Hosts Media Call Today - NASA
item.description: The first mission devoted to observing the Martian atmosphere and its evolution, NASA’s MAVEN (Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution), has ended after more

item.title: NASA Awards Modification Contract for Reduced Gravity Test Aircraft - NASA
item.description: The aircraft will be used to validate astronaut lunar suits and associated crew systems required to support Artemis mission objectives. This can be done with

item.title: 2026 News Releases - NASA
item.description: Sign up to receive NASA news releases and other information.

item.title: Human Research, Biotechnology for Advanced Health Fill Station Research Schedule - NASA
item.description: Wednesday’s science schedule aboard the International Space Station encompassed human research, microbial analysis, and 3D bioprinting to advance health on and off the Earth. The Expedition 74 crew members also worked on spacesuit maintenance and cargo spacecraft duties throughout the day rounding out their shift.

item.title: Bacteria, Cartilage, and Metal Tops Tuesday’s Research Aboard Station - NASA
item.description: Microbiology, biotechnology, and physics were the dominant research themes aboard the International Space Station on Tuesday as the Expedition 74 crew explored how microgravity affects bacteria, cartilage growth, and metallic structure. Scientific hardware maintenance rounded out the schedule for the orbital residents to ensure continuous experimental operations.

item.title: News | Rocket Lab
item.description: Keep up with the latest from Rocket Lab.

item.title: Rocket Lab Awarded $90M Contract to Build GEO Satellites Hosting Space Domain Awareness Payload for U.S. Space Force | Rocket Lab
item.description: Award marks Rocket Lab's first satellite production program for geostationary orbit and continues a Space Systems Command program for development and delivery on orbit of two Heimdall prototype payloads by Rocket Lab Optical Systems.

item.title: Rocket Lab Adds Mars-Proven Robotics Capabilities with Completion of Motiv Space Systems Acquisition | Rocket Lab
item.description: Acquisition brings Mars heritage for complex planetary missions, as well as precision mechanisms essential for next-generation space infrastructure including orbital data centers and constellations

item.title: Mission Success: Rocket Lab Completes 9th Electron Launch for Synspective | Rocket Lab
item.description: Mahia, New Zealand – Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today announced the successful deployment of the latest satellite for Synspective, marking the ninth dedicated mission Rocket Lab has completed for the Japan-based Earth observation…

item.title: Rocket Lab Awarded $30 Million Contract for HASTE Hypersonic Rocket Launches for Anduril | Rocket Lab
item.description: Long Beach, California. May 7, 2026 – Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today announced it has been selected by defense technology company Anduril Industries for multiple hypersonic test flights with its HASTE launch vehicle. The…

item.title: Rocket Lab’s Biggest Launch Deal Yet: Confidential Customer Books Multiple Neutron and Electron Launches | Rocket Lab
item.description: The multi-launch contract includes dedicated missions on five Neutron and three Electron rockets for a confidential customer with the launches baselined for 2026-2029

item.title: April 2026 Company Update
item.description: April brought continued momentum across the Terran R program, with progress advancing across design, build, test, and launch.  Design This month, 2,055 flight parts were released, with a concerted effort on the stage one shipping assembly. Nearly all of the first stage structural compone

item.title: March 2026 Company Update
item.description: March brought continued progress across the Terran R program, with key milestones across design, manufacturing, testing, and launch preparations.  Design This month, the team released 2,207 flight parts, with a continued focus on completing the stage one shipping assembly. Work remains c

item.title: February 2026 Company Update
item.description: February marked another month of critical progress across design, build, test, and launch. Design This month, the team released an additional 1,826 flight components. The stage two shipping assembly and stage two fluids top-level assembly are now fully released, enabling final integration activit

item.title: January 2026 Company Update
item.description: January marked a strong start to the year, with meaningful progress across design, build, test, and launch. Design This month, the team released 1,856 flight parts. At this stage of the program, part releases are primarily for secondary structures and integration hardware that will be installed l
